sprints.name: Polish & Quick Wins
sprints.rationale: P1 items and fast visual wins — closes open UX gaps and knocks out the QA backlog before hardening begins.

items.title: Unified Notification Hub — Merge NotificationBell + TaskPanel
items.notes:
Current fragmentation: NotificationBell (top right), TaskPanel (processing queue), inline toasts, email queue. Create unified notification center with categories: Actions Needed (approvals, requests), System Events (audits complete), Alerts (score drops). Add notification preferences per workspace.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] NotificationBell and TaskPanel both ship as separate components rendered side-by-side; no unified hub merges their categories.

items.title: CMS Editor UX Redesign — Match SeoEditor simplicity
items.notes:
CmsEditor UI/UX is noticeably rougher than the clean static-page SeoEditor. Redesign to match: consistent card layout, inline field editing, clear save/publish flow, per-collection grouping with search/filter. Consider shared editing primitives between CmsEditor and SeoEditor to prevent future drift.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] CmsEditor.tsx already shares SeoEditor primitives (CharacterCounter, SerpPreview, SocialPreview, StatusBadge, statusBorderClass) and the PendingApprovals panel; full layout-parity redesign vs SeoEditor is unverified.

items.title: Stripe payment modal — visual QA in test mode (light + dark)
items.notes: Superseded 2026-05-04 by platform-consolidation-billing-checkout-only. The inline Stripe Elements payment modal and StripePaymentForm were removed; client purchases now redirect to hosted Stripe Checkout, so there is no in-app Elements modal to visually QA.

items.title: Mobile breakpoint visual smoke — Phase 5 token migration
items.source: Phase 5 Phase 2 follow-up — staging smoke gap
items.notes:
Phase 2 codemod batches touched ~225 components but the original staging smoke test on 2026-04-25 only exercised desktop breakpoints — the resize_window MCP call didn't take effect on the Chrome instance. Tailwind responsive utilities are unchanged at the codemod level (only color tokens swapped, not breakpoint prefixes), but token swaps at breakpoint-conditional className boundaries could have edge cases. Test at 375 (mobile), 640 (sm), 768 (md), 1024 (lg) breakpoints across: WorkspaceOverview (admin home), ClientDashboard (overview/strategy/content tabs), SeoAudit (audit results table), Settings (Workspace + Client tabs). Look for: stacked vs grid layouts, button sizes, table overflow, sidebar collapse. Risk if skipped: medium — a regression at one breakpoint is invisible on desktop testing.
[Shipped 2026-05-15] Added repeatable Playwright harness `tests/playwright/visual/wave5-mobile-smoke.spec.ts` to verify those exact routes and breakpoints against staging. First run caught 375px horizontal overflow on client overview/strategy/content routes; fixed via responsive header wrapping + root x-overflow containment in `ClientHeader` and `ClientDashboard`.

items.title: Harden flaky brand-engine + brand-identity integration tests
items.source: CI flakiness — recurring on staging push runs
items.notes: Fixed with a four-layer defense: (1) waitForServer health-check polling added to startServer() in tests/integration/helpers.ts so tests never fire before routes are serving; (2) SQLite busy_timeout increased from 5s to 30s to handle macOS APFS fsync stalls under parallel load; (3) incrementIfAllowed moved inside try/catch in all async AI handlers (brand-identity.ts, voice-calibration.ts) to prevent SQLITE_BUSY from escaping as an unhandled promise rejection that crashes the server; (4) global JSON error handler added to app.ts so synchronous throws return JSON 500 instead of HTML. All write transactions in voice-calibration.ts changed to .immediate() to prevent SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT in WAL mode. Pre-commit hook scoped to unit tests only to avoid parallel SQLite contention during local commits.

items.title: Schema: Write generated schema to CMS collection field
items.notes: Implemented first pass: collection-aware siteInventory, CMS collection role inference/mapping, synthetic CMS page IDs in single-page generation, utility-page bulk exclusions, diagnostics with collection identity/evidence/CMS delivery status, and JSON-only CMS field publishing through mapped collection fields such as schema-json-ld. Follow-up quality pass added persisted Detect + Map bindings for location/service fields, human-readable reference resolution, Service.offers from verified price/currency, safer homepage LocalBusiness gating, FAQ index suppression, and clearer schema-vs-CMS diagnostics. Remaining polish: broader collection role test coverage and deeper Webflow reference dereferencing where the API only returns IDs.

items.title: Form Validation Standardization — React Hook Form + Zod
items.notes: Shipped 2026-05-11: added React Hook Form + @hookform/resolvers as the standard client form stack; extended shared FormField/FormInput/FormSelect/FormTextarea primitives with error-authoritative valid/success state, emerald valid borders, aria-invalid, aria-describedby, alert/status messages; migrated LoginScreen and EmailCaptureGate from basic/manual validation to Zod-backed forms with inline field errors, disabled-invalid submits, loading states, and trimmed submit values. Focused tests cover primitive valid/error precedence, client email validation/submission, and admin login behavior. Remaining form migrations should be tracked as specific follow-up component items rather than this broad standardization item.

items.title: Loading State Consistency — Unified skeleton system
items.notes:
Standardize loading patterns: route-level (keep ChunkFallback), data fetching (skeleton screens), actions (button loading + optimistic UI), background jobs (progress panel).
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] Skeleton primitive widely adopted (169 usages across 34 files including Roadmap, ClientDashboard, AdminInbox, brand/*, client/*); no formal standardization rule, lint, or codified loading-pattern doc.
Shipped / re-audited 2026-05-06: loading-state standardization now exists through shared Skeleton, LoadingState, SectionCardSkeleton, and ProgressIndicator primitives; route-level ChunkFallback remains in App.tsx, background jobs use TaskPanel/useBackgroundTasks, and docs/rules/ui-ux-consistency.md codifies contextual loading and skeleton usage. Remaining future polish can be tracked as specific component migrations rather than this broad standardization item.

items.title: Blog/content editor improvements — TipTap rich text, auto-save, reading time, AI Fix This flow
items.notes: Shipped — Phase 1: replaced all PostEditor/SectionEditor/PostReviewCard textareas with TipTap v3 rich text editor (bold/italic/H2/H3/links via BubbleMenu). New useAutoSave hook (debounced 2s, stale-closure-safe). Reading time added to PostPreview. Phase 2: AI 'Fix This' button on failed ReviewChecklist items. POST /api/content-posts/:wsId/:postId/ai-fix with 6 issue-key strategies (factual_accuracy, brand_voice, internal_links, no_hallucinations, meta_optimized, word_count_target). FixDiffModal shows two-column before/after diff. AiFixResult + IssueKey shared types. POST_UPDATED broadcast added to PATCH handler. 6 unit tests (useAutoSave) + 6 integration tests (ai-fix endpoint).

items.title: CI runtime optimization — path-filtered jobs, concurrency cancellation, and Playwright cache
items.notes: Shipped in .github/workflows/ci.yml and .github/workflows/e2e.yml. Added workflow-level concurrency cancel-in-progress to avoid duplicate runs per branch/PR. Split CI into change-detection + conditional quality/test/coverage jobs, moved coverage off PRs to push-only, and added required-check aggregation so branch protections stay stable. Added file-change gating via dorny/paths-filter for both CI and E2E so unchanged docs/non-runtime edits skip heavy jobs. Added Playwright browser cache keyed by Playwright version to avoid re-downloading browsers on every run. Follow-up optimization: E2E now runs in 2 Playwright shards (`--shard=1/2` and `--shard=2/2`) with a stable top-level `e2e` aggregator check so branch protection wiring stays unchanged. Additional follow-up: E2E now builds frontend once (`e2e-build` artifact) and reuses the shared `dist/` artifact in shards, and shard blob outputs are merged into a single `playwright-report-merged` artifact for easier debugging. Additional CI follow-up: PR Vitest execution now runs as a 3-way shard matrix (`--shard=1/3`, `2/3`, `3/3`) with a stable top-level `test` aggregator check to preserve required-check naming while reducing test wall-clock time.

Added `src/api/platform.ts` (jobs/roadmap/features/notifications/workspace-home/observability surfaces) and `src/api/schema.ts` (schema/schema-validation/schema-plan/schema-impact surfaces), then converted `src/api/misc.ts` and `src/api/seo.ts` into compatibility re-export facades for those moved wrappers. items.title: Architecture Drift Checks
items.notes:
Shipped 2026-05-15: implemented styleguide drift architecture guardrails with a canonical lockdown contract (`docs/rules/styleguide-lockdown-contract.md`), new pr-check enforcement rules (style exception metadata contract, raw `<button>` drift detector, raw CTA literal detector), warning→error ratchet promotions for zero-backlog style rules, and a machine-readable style drift report/baseline regression gate (`scripts/report-style-drift.ts`, `verify:style-drift`, `report:style-drift`).
Wave follow-up 2026-05-15: completed a parallel admin action-button primitive sweep across SEO Editor action rows (`SeoEditorHeaderActions`, `ApprovalPanel`, `SeoEditorTableControls`, `BulkOperations`), SettingsPanel top-level controls, WorkspaceHome action links/status cards, and SchemaSuggester top controls. Style drift metrics improved from `raw_button_unallowlisted_count: 891 -> 855` and `non_primitive_action_count: 876 -> 840`; admin raw-button count now `717` (down from `753`).
Wave 3b follow-up 2026-05-15: migrated admin settings/audit controls in `ConnectionsTab`, `FeaturesTab`, `ClientDashboardTab`, and SEO Audit share actions to `Button`/`IconButton`. Style drift metrics improved further to `raw_button_unallowlisted_count: 808` and `non_primitive_action_count: 793`; admin raw-button count now `670`.
Wave 4 follow-up 2026-05-17: established canonical styleguide parity rule registry artifacts (`docs/rules/styleguide-rule-registry.md`, `data/styleguide-rule-registry.json`) with rule-level enforcement tiers (`error|warn|manual`), ownership, metric keys, and promotion prerequisites to guide future ratchet waves from advisory to blocking checks.
Wave 5 follow-up 2026-05-17: added three non-blocking parity detectors in `pr-check` (`styleguide-css-must-import-public-tokens`, `styleguide-typography-extra-class-drift`, `global-token-declaration-outside-canonical-token-files`) with fixture coverage in `tests/pr-check.test.ts` so token/typography drift surfaces as advisory before promotion.
Wave 8 follow-up 2026-05-18: promoted five zero-backlog parity rules to blocking errors (`hardcoded card radius outside ui primitives`, `badge-like-span-outside-primitives`, `styleguide-css-must-import-public-tokens`, `styleguide-typography-extra-class-drift`, `global-token-declaration-outside-canonical-token-files`), added warn-tier detectors (`src-index-css-no-token-declarations`, `badge-color-prop-deprecation`, `interactive-div-role-button`, `primitive-override-drift-on-form-controls`), and migrated the final four live badge-like spans to `Badge` primitives in client surfaces.
Wave 10 follow-up 2026-05-17: removed structural class overrides from shared form primitive callsites across client/admin/editor surfaces, tightened `primitive-override-drift-on-form-controls` to structural drift only, promoted that rule from warn to error with docs/registry sync, and reached zero backlog (`raw form control advisory: admin=0, client=0, total=0`).

items.notes: Shipped 2026-05-16: added `scripts/platform-risky-module-dashboard.ts` risk analyzer and `scripts/report-risky-modules.ts` CLI wrapper. The dashboard ranks modules by weighted risk signals: line count, 90/180-day git churn, internal import fan-in/fan-out, TODO/FIXME/HACK + `*-ok` hatch density, test linkage, route write handlers lacking nearby integration tests, and `pr-check` warning-hit concentration. Added package commands (`verify:risky-modules`, `report:risky-modules`), integrated the audit into `scripts/verify-platform.ts`, documented workflow in `docs/workflows/risky-module-dashboard.md`, and added unit coverage in `tests/unit/platform-risky-module-dashboard.test.ts`.

items.title: AI Research Mode — Anti-Hallucination Prompt Blocks
items.source: USER REQUEST (Reddit find: Anthropic docs anti-hallucination techniques)
items.notes:
Add toggleable 'research mode' prompt blocks to AI features that make factual claims. Three techniques from Anthropic docs: (1) Allow 'I don't know' — stops confident-sounding fiction. (2) Verify with citations — retract unsourced claims. (3) Direct quotes for factual grounding — extract verbatim before analyzing. Apply to: content briefs (competitor insights, SERP analysis), SEO audit AI recommendations, content post generation, admin chat. Do NOT apply to creative features (SEO rewrites, keyword brainstorming) — research shows citation constraints reduce creative output. Implementation: add researchMode flag to callOpenAI opts, inject prompt blocks conditionally.
[Hardening 2026-05-13 partial] Added internal callAI researchMode support for OpenAI/Anthropic and applied it to factual content brief, post generation, post review, and provenance-sensitive AI-fix paths. Also threads stored SERP evidence into post context and surfaces numeric claims for reviewer attention. SEO audit recommendations/admin chat remain pending for this broader roadmap item.

items.title: Content Review Evidence Panel — Show SERP provenance for factual claims
items.notes: Shipped 2026-05-14 in Output Quality Follow-Ups PR. Post review now loads the source brief and displays stored `realPeopleAlsoAsk` and `realTopResults` in the existing review checklist as reviewer support. AI review still marks factual accuracy/no hallucinations as human-review-only and never auto-checks provenance-sensitive items.

items.title: Internal Link Recommendation Source Audit
items.notes: Follow-up from backend hardening PR 5. Audit whether internal link recommendations are fed from one canonical source or multiple sources (internal link scanner, AI recommendations, strategy/content surfaces, approval payloads). Document the owning source of truth, data contracts, and any drift between admin generation, client review, and intelligence/chat context before consolidating implementation.

items.title: Internal Link Client Review Display Model Cleanup
items.notes: Follow-up from backend hardening PR 5. Current client review display can show target URL as a page title and source page as a slug, which makes review confusing. Preserve reviewer-support wording and avoid changing the broader approval workflow.

items.title: Audit Snapshot Effective Summary Query Optimization
items.notes: Follow-up from audit intelligence hardening PR 5. listEffectiveSnapshotSummaries now preserves suppression-adjusted score/history correctness by loading full snapshots when suppressions exist, but that can become N+1-heavy for long audit histories and workspace report-list reads. Add an efficient table-backed summary/read model or bounded cached effective-history helper that preserves suppression-adjusted current/history semantics without repeatedly parsing full audit JSON blobs.

items.title: E-E-A-T Asset Library — Personalized Trust Signal Recommendations
items.notes: Current E-E-A-T guidance in content briefs is generic per-pillar text ('include case studies'). Gap: no inventory of what assets a client ACTUALLY has. Build: (1) Workspace-level E-E-A-T profile in settings — admin tags existing client assets: testimonials (with quotes + attribution), case studies (with URLs + metrics), certifications/credentials, before/after galleries, team bios/expertise, awards/recognition, data/research, client logos. (2) Content briefs inject specific asset recommendations: 'Add the Dr. Smith testimonial from /reviews' or 'Include the Acme Corp case study showing 40% conversion lift'. (3) Page Intelligence flags pages missing trust signals based on page type (service pages need testimonials, product pages need reviews, etc.). (4) Schema generator uses asset library for author/credential structured data. New DB table: eeat_assets (workspace_id, type, title, url, content, metadata JSON). New UI: settings tab or workspace profile section.

items.title: Schema Workstream C — Entity Graph Hub Dispatch
items.notes: Workstream C shipped: hub pages now emit semantically correct schema types with cross-page @id references via assembleSiteContext + SiteContext plumbing into generateLeanSchema. BlogIndex → Blog with blogPost[] (@id refs, sorted newest-first, capped at 10, numberOfItems = true count). ServiceIndex → Service + hasOfferCatalog: { @type: OfferCatalog, hasPart[] }. CaseStudyIndex → CollectionPage + mainEntity: { @type: ItemList, itemListElement[] (positional ListItems) }. All three fall back to CollectionPage when siteContext is absent. Non-hub pages (BlogPosting, Service, AboutPage, etc.) unchanged. 18 integration tests in tests/integration/schema-entity-graph.test.ts.

items.title: Schema Semantic Extraction & AI-Powered Generation
items.notes: AI-powered universal page content extraction layer for schema generation. extractSemanticData() (Haiku 4.5 tool_use) extracts phone, address, hours, staff, services, aggregateRating, sameAs, faq, primaryImage per page — stored on PageElementCatalog.semantics. applyPostEnrichment() appends FAQPage/AggregateRating/sameAs/VideoObject nodes to generated schema using extracted semantics with rollback-on-new-errors safety. generateSchemaForUnknownType() generates WebPage-classified pages with non-empty semantics via second Haiku call + static SCHEMA_TYPE_REFERENCE. Validator extended with DEDICATED_VALIDATOR_TYPES guard, unverified-type warnings for unknown Schema.org types, addressCountry in ContactPage. GSC URL Inspection integration: inspectUrlForRichResults() calls GSC API 3 minutes after publish, stores google_validation_status on schema_publish_history. schema.org pre-publish validator: validateWithSchemaOrg() fires at generation time (fire-and-forget, logs only). Migrations 081 (google_validation_status on schema_publish_history) + 082 (schema_org_validation_status on schema_snapshots). WS_EVENTS.SCHEMA_GOOGLE_VALIDATION added. 9 new/modified test files; 5805 tests passing.

items.title: Schema Page-Element Catalog PR3 — polish (codeBlocks, headings/ToC, speakable)
items.notes: Optional polish: codeBlocks → CodeRepository/SoftwareSourceCode (low ROI for agency sites); headings → WebPage.speakable cssSelector (overlaps with schema-engagement-signals; whichever ships first wins). May be skipped if cycles are short.

items.title: Migrate buildSchemaContext direct reads to intelligence slices
items.notes: Completed 2026-05-07. buildSchemaContext now reads schema context from `buildWorkspaceIntelligence({ slices: ['seoContext'] })` for businessContext, knowledgeBase, personas, siteKeywords, and business profile (including structured address + foundedDate/numberOfEmployees via seoContext.businessProfile). Legacy `ctx.brandVoice` assignment was removed as dead schema context. Remaining direct reads are identity-only (`ws.name`, `ws.id`, `ws.liveDomain`, `ws.brandLogoUrl`, `ws.siteHasSearch`, `_siteId`) plus analytics maps, matching the allowlist intent behind `schema-context-direct-read-not-on-allowlist`.

items.title: Remove dead pageKeywordMap from buildSchemaContext + generateSchemaSuggestions signature
items.notes: After schema-yoast-parity-fields PR1, buildSchemaContext still computes pageKeywordMap from ws?.keywordStrategy?.pageMap and returns it (server/helpers.ts:343, :410-418, :539). Both callers (server/routes/webflow-schema.ts:51, server/routes/jobs.ts:636) destructure it and pass to generateSchemaSuggestions, which immediately voids it (server/schema-suggester.ts:391). Per-page keywords are now fetched via buildWorkspaceIntelligence inside the page loop. Cleanup: drop the pageKeywordMap return field, the parameter, and the void discard. While in there, audit the other 5 voided params on generateSchemaSuggestions (gscMap, ga4Map, queryPageData, insightsMap, validationsByPageId) — likely also dead. Devin Review explicitly recommended deferring to a follow-up PR; doesn't affect correctness.

items.title: Schema engagement signals — wordCount, timeRequired, speakable, educationalLevel
items.notes: Article.wordCount shipped in the Blog/Article Schema Quality Hardening pass (May 2026) using visible content extraction instead of full articleBody duplication. Remaining quick-win Tier-4 fields with data the platform already has: Article.timeRequired = wordCount/250wpm rounded; WebPage.speakable with cssSelector pointing to .lead/.intro/first-paragraph — voice-assistant rich result; Article.educationalLevel via AI classification (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced). All wire through existing PageData typed boundary. No new admin UI required for the basics; speakable cssSelector is a per-template constant. Independent rich-result eligibility unlock for Google Article + voice-search amplification.

items.title: Schema E-E-A-T amplifiers — publishingPrinciples, reviewedBy, copyrightHolder, isAccessibleForFree, Person.jobTitle
items.notes: Tier-3 trust signals Google increasingly weighs. Add 4 new optional Workspace fields: publishingPrinciplesUrl, actionableFeedbackPolicyUrl (both → Organization.publishingPrinciples + actionableFeedbackPolicy on every page's @graph). Article.copyrightHolder ref to Organization, copyrightYear from datePublished. Article.reviewedBy as Person from new CMS field 'fact_checker' (optional). Article.isAccessibleForFree from per-tier content gating. Person.jobTitle + worksFor on author when CMS author records have those fields (currently they're just string names). Mostly admin Settings UI work + template emission. Each field is an additive E-E-A-T signal — non-breaking, immediate ranking benefit on YMYL content.

items.title: Schema entity grounding — typed Thing/Place entities + Wikidata sameAs disambiguation
items.notes: Tier-2 / biggest competitive moat. Replace string knowsAbout (currently ['brand strategy','webflow']) with typed Thing entities linked to Wikidata Q-numbers via sameAs. Same upgrade for Article.about (typed Thing — what is this article about?), Article.mentions[] (entities referenced in body), Place entities for areaServed (Austin, TX → Wikidata Q16559), Organization.sameAs extended with Wikidata identifier. Architecture: new EntityResolutionSlice on buildWorkspaceIntelligence, populated by a Cheerio-based extractor that runs entity recognition (likely via OpenAI structured-output prompt against curated entity types) + Wikidata SPARQL lookup for top candidates, cached per-entity. Most agencies don't do this; Google's knowledge graph rewards disambiguation heavily. Architectural prerequisite met by parity-fields PR1 Pattern B starter. Can ship after schema-page-element-catalog-v1 (the catalog feeds Article.mentions extraction).

items.title: Schema commerce types — Service.offers (PriceSpecification), Product, Course, Event
items.notes: Tier-5 monetization opportunity for agencies with productized services / paid content. Service.offers with Offer { price, priceCurrency, availability } — Google's 'service prices' SERP feature. Product schema for templates/digital products. Course for educational content/training. Event for webinars/launches with eventStatus + eventAttendanceMode (Online/Offline/Mixed). Each is independent rich-result eligibility unlock. Workspace.contentPricing already exists (used for content-purchase tiers); Service.offers can reuse the pricing infra. Per-type effort similar to parity-fields PR1's per-template additions; parallelizable across template files. Default-off; admin opts in per-workspace. Most useful for agencies with packaged services (web-design + brand audit + content retainer); skip for service-only sites without published prices.

items.title: Schema trust/authority — Organization.award, member, hasCredential, numberOfEmployees
items.notes: Tier-6 reputation graph fields. New Workspace.organizationAwards (string[] of Award names → Award entity[]), Workspace.organizationMemberships (industry orgs → ProgramMembership/Organization references), Workspace.organizationCredentials (certifications → EducationalOccupationalCredential), Workspace.numberOfEmployees (QuantitativeValue { minValue, maxValue, unitText: 'employee' }). Each emitted on Organization sibling node in homepage + local-business templates. Pure admin Settings UI + template extension; no AI/intelligence dependencies. Agency credibility in SERP, especially for B2B competitive queries.

items.title: Reset aiLimiter in beforeEach for brandscript-hardening I14 burst-cap
items.notes: Verified during 2026-05-03 flake-hardening pass: no duplicate reset hook needed. brandscript-hardening and brand-identity I14 already isolate aiLimiter by creating a fresh workspace per test, and rateLimit keys include req.path with the workspaceId. Targeted run passed alongside the other flake files.

items.title: Add missing exports to outcome-tracking.js mock factory
items.notes: Fixed during 2026-05-03 flake-hardening pass. Added stable no-op getActionsByWorkspace/getPendingActions/getTopWinsFromActions coverage to outcome-tracking mocks used by workspace-intelligence-related tests so dynamic imports no longer see partial mock shapes. Targeted run passed.

items.title: Investigate content-decay-routes graceful-degradation timeout in full-suite mode
items.notes: Fixed during 2026-05-03 flake-hardening pass. The test server now inherits OPENAI_API_KEY='' so callOpenAI fails synchronously through the missing-key guard instead of retrying a fake provider key. The route still returns 200 with fallback recommendations. Targeted run passed.

items.title: Schema Pillar 2.1 — Stop emitting SearchAction on sites without search
items.notes: Pillar 2's homepage + LocalBusiness templates emitted `potentialAction: SearchAction` unconditionally. Google's docs require the site actually expose a working search endpoint at the urlTemplate; emitting it on a site without search misrepresents capability. Fix: dropped SearchAction emission until a workspace-level signal indicates site search is available. Re-add as opt-in via Workspace.siteHasSearch (or auto-detect from homepage HTML <form action=/search>) is queued under schema-yoast-parity-fields.

items.title: Schema Pillar 1 — Raise the Validator Bar
items.notes: REQUIRED_BY_TYPE in validator.ts now enforces Yoast/Google-baseline completeness. Article/BlogPosting require image+dateModified+articleSection+isPartOf+breadcrumb+inLanguage. WebPage variants require description+isPartOf+breadcrumb+inLanguage. LocalBusiness requires address+telephone+inLanguage. WebSite requires inLanguage (potentialAction is conditional, deferred to schema-yoast-parity-fields where it gates on Workspace.siteHasSearch). Organization requires logo. Cross-ref validators reject malformed isPartOf/breadcrumb shapes and dangling BreadcrumbList @id pointers. NEW value-shape validators reject malformed Article author/publisher/image, non-ISO 8601 dates, non-contiguous breadcrumb positions, and non-absolute URLs on primary nodes. Locks the bar Pillars 2 + 2.1 raised. 24 new test assertions across required-fields, cross-ref shape, and value-shape describe blocks.

items.title: Schema Yoast-Parity Fields PR1 — validator API + 6 fields + slice-migration starter
items.notes: ValidationFinding API (typed { severity, type, field?, ruleId, message }) + 6 new template fields (Service.areaServed, Service.serviceType, LocalBusiness.areaServed, Organization.knowsAbout, Article.keywords, gated WebSite.potentialAction) + Workspace.siteHasSearch field plumbing (default false) + slice-migration starter (siteKeywords + per-page pageKeywords moved to seoContext slice consumption) + new pr-check rule schema-context-direct-read-not-on-allowlist + CLAUDE.md convention paragraph + roadmap migration tracker entry (schema-context-builder-pattern-b-migration). 5 dead SchemaContext._* fields deleted (3 preserved per audit). Frontend renders findings grouped by severity (errors red, warnings amber 'Recommended'). PR2 ships admin discoverability surfaces. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-29-schema-yoast-parity-fields-design.md. Audit: docs/superpowers/audits/2026-04-29-schema-yoast-parity-fields-audit.md.

items.title: Schema Yoast-Parity Fields PR2 — admin discoverability surfaces
items.notes: Admin discoverability layer atop PR1's typed validationFindings. SchemaCompletenessWidget on the Schema page aggregates findings across all pages with progress bar + missing-field deep-link buttons. BusinessProfileTab gains a 'Schema impact' read-only mirror SectionCard listing 5 schema-impacting fields with ✓/✗ status + deep-links (cross-tab via Link with ?tab=...&focus=..., in-tab via scroll-to-element). FeaturesTab gains Logo URL microcopy + new 'Site capabilities' SectionCard with the siteHasSearch checkbox (toggling on activates PR1's gated WebSite.potentialAction emission). SchemaPageCard now groups findings by field with click-to-expand collapsible rows when a single field has 2+ findings on a page. SchemaSuggester header gains 'X fixes available' annotation. New shared useDeepLinkFocus hook + data-schema-deeplink attribute convention establishes the field-level deep-link two-halves contract. New fieldTargets.ts module is the single source of truth mapping ValidationFinding.field → Settings deep-link. Frontend-only; no backend changes. 8 new component tests (3 hook + 5 widget). Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-29-schema-yoast-parity-fields-pr2.md.

items.title: Schema Pillar 3 — Compile-time + CI Gates
items.notes: DEFERRED. Pillar 3 is regression-prevention hardening (schema-dts compile-time types + schemarama CI corpus + pr-check rule for cross-ref helper imports). It does not change schema output — it locks the bar Pillars 1 + 2 set. Decision (2026-04-29): defer until after schema-yoast-parity-fields ships, because the field expansion will reshape templates and Pillar 3's typed signatures would have to be rewritten. Reactivate after intelligence layer settles. Plan file remains committed for reference.

items.title: Workspace onboarding automation — templates, batch setup, reduce repetitive per-workspace tasks

Most steps are identical across workspaces. Add: workspace templates (clone config from existing workspace), batch analysis trigger (run audit + keyword analysis + decay analysis in one click), onboarding checklist with progress tracking, default competitor auto-discovery. Separate initiative from intelligence engine — scoped as its own spec.

items.title: Schema Intelligence Layer (FAQ-from-GSC, E-E-A-T, healthcare subtypes)
items.notes: Follow-up to lean-schema-rewrite. Re-injects the platform's intelligence layer surgically into schema generation: (1) Healthcare type escalation — workspace businessContext → Dentist/Physician/Optician/Chiropractor/MedicalClinic/etc. when address+phone+hours are present, falls back to LocalBusiness otherwise. (2) FAQ enrichment from GSC question queries — extractFaqOpportunities (already exists in analytics-intelligence.ts) feeds FAQPage when ≥3 question queries match a page. (3) E-E-A-T author injection from content brief — extractEeatFromBrief (already exists) populates Article.author with Person.knowsAbout, sameAs from the brief's expert profile. (4) Service serviceType from voice profile + business profile. Reuses existing intelligence wiring; the lean rewrite kept the data flow intact, this task adds surgical injection points to the templates.

items.title: InboxTab Phase 3.3 — Per-item exclusion in approval batches

items.title: InboxTab Phase 3.4 — Client signals resolution (loop engagement → insight engine)
items.notes: Client actions approved/completed should feed back into the insight engine: mark the originating insight as resolved, update ClientSignalsSlice engagement metrics, and credit the win in WinsSurface. Currently approval data is siloed from the intelligence layer.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — Platform Health Wave 5 — Product & Lifecycle Discipline
sprints.rationale: Keep the platform understandable as it grows by managing product surface area, feature flags, deprecations, performance budgets, local onboarding, ADRs, and platform-health cadence.

items.title: Product Surface Rationalization
items.notes: Shipped Wave 5 product-surface governance baseline. Added typed capability map + report script (`scripts/product-surface-map.ts`) with placement/role/lifecycle/recommendation taxonomy, canonical bounded-context coverage checks, and explicit human-verification queue for non-obvious nav/deprecation decisions. Added test coverage in `tests/unit/product-surface-map.test.ts` and rule doc `docs/rules/product-surface-rationalization.md` that codifies manual approval gates. Added `npm run verify:product-surface` command and documented the capability in FEATURE_AUDIT entry 418.

items.title: Feature Flag Lifecycle Management
items.notes: Shipped Wave 5 feature-flag lifecycle management baseline. Expanded `shared/types/feature-flags.ts` into a single source-of-truth catalog with typed lifecycle metadata (owner, createdAt, rolloutTarget, removalCondition, linkedRoadmapItemId, staleAuditCadence, lastReviewedAt), centralized admin grouping/labels, and shared API contract type `FeatureFlagAdminMeta`. Updated server/admin surfaces so `/api/admin/feature-flags` now returns lifecycle context for each flag and FeatureFlagSettings renders that metadata inline. Added lifecycle audit command `npm run verify:feature-flags` (`scripts/feature-flag-lifecycle.ts`) plus test coverage (`tests/unit/feature-flag-lifecycle.test.ts`) and refreshed docs (`docs/rules/feature-flag-lifecycle.md`, development/release-safety references).

items.title: Feature Flag Promotion & Sunset Pass
items.notes: Shipped Wave 5 promotion/sunset pass for low-risk active flags validated on staging. Promoted and removed `client-wins-surface` and `seo-editor-unified` from `shared/types/feature-flags.ts` and lifecycle catalog metadata; removed runtime gates/fallback branches so promoted paths are now canonical (`InsightsBriefingPage` always renders `WinsSurface`; `SeoEditorWrapper` always renders unified editor). Updated contract/component tests and feature audit wording to remove stale flag references.

items.title: Deprecation Lifecycle Discipline
items.notes: Shipped Wave 5 deprecation lifecycle governance baseline. Added typed registry + audit command `npm run verify:deprecations` (`scripts/deprecation-lifecycle.ts`) covering lifecycle states (`deprecated`, `hidden`, `read-only`, `migrated`, `removed`) with state-specific policy checks and required code/test evidence links. Added test coverage (`tests/unit/deprecation-lifecycle.test.ts`) and rule doc `docs/rules/deprecation-lifecycle.md` to codify operating flow and human-verification requirements for deprecated/migrated/removed surfaces.

items.title: Platform Performance Budgets
items.notes: Shipped Wave 5 platform performance budget governance baseline. Added typed performance budget registry + verification command (`npm run verify:performance-budgets`) in `scripts/performance-budgets.ts` covering per-workflow AI call caps, external fetch caps, route response targets, query count budgets, expected background-job durations, cache expectations, and escalation triggers/actions. Seeded high-risk workflows across jobs and public/admin read paths with required code/test evidence references and policy validation checks (duplicate ids, numeric budget integrity, evidence path existence, background-job duration requirements). Added rule doc `docs/rules/performance-budgets.md` and unit coverage in `tests/unit/performance-budgets.test.ts`.

items.title: Local Dev & Agent Onboarding Confidence
items.notes: Shipped Wave 5 local onboarding confidence baseline. Added deterministic local fake-provider mode (`LOCAL_FAKE_PROVIDERS=true`) for SEO providers plus synthetic AI responses in OpenAI/Anthropic helpers so first-hour setup works without external keys. Added one-command demo workspace seeding (`npm run seed:demo`, script: `scripts/seed-demo-workspaces.ts`) and one-command core smoke bundle (`npm run smoke:core`). Documented onboarding workflow + environment tiers in `docs/workflows/local-dev-onboarding.md`, linked from codebase overview, and added unit coverage for local mode + fake provider contracts.

items.title: Recurring Platform Health Sprint Cadence
items.notes: Shipped recurring platform-health cadence contract. Added source-of-truth registry (`data/platform-health-cadence.json`) with 4-6 sprint cadence policy, measurable dimensions, checkpoint evidence links, and Wave 5 baseline metrics. Added verifier command `npm run verify:platform-health-cadence` (`scripts/platform-health-cadence.ts`) with policy/roadmap-link checks, due-window computation, and unit coverage (`tests/unit/platform-health-cadence.test.ts`). Added workflow playbook `docs/workflows/platform-health-cadence.md` and wired cadence audit into `verify-platform`.

items.title: Architecture Decision Record Log
items.notes: Shipped lightweight ADR governance baseline. Added ADR home + six seed architecture decisions in `docs/adr/` covering background jobs, workspace-intelligence slices, feature-flag lifecycle/sunset, client/admin route split, unified AI dispatch, and bounded-context service extraction. Added verifier command `npm run verify:adr-log` (`scripts/adr-log.ts`) with required-section and required-topic coverage checks plus unit tests (`tests/unit/adr-log.test.ts`). Added workflow playbook `docs/workflows/adr-log.md` and wired ADR verification into `verify-platform`.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — Platform Health Wave 4 — Runtime & Operational Confidence
sprints.rationale: Improve runtime confidence: know when integrations fail, evaluate AI quality, recover data safely, observe operations, and protect tenant boundaries.

items.title: Integration Health Center
items.notes: Shipped 2026-05-15: added a typed workspace-scoped Integration Health Center contract (`shared/types/integration-health.ts`), backend aggregator endpoint (`GET /api/integrations/health/:workspaceId`) in `server/routes/health.ts`, and frontend Connections-tab surface with React Query wiring (`useIntegrationHealth`, `queryKeys.admin.integrationHealth`). Health cards now report configured/missing/degraded state for Webflow, Google auth, GSC, GA4, SEMRush, DataForSEO, Stripe, OpenAI, Anthropic, and SMTP, including quota-status placeholders, token expiry visibility for Google auth, and affected feature mapping. Added integration endpoint coverage (`tests/integration/health-routes.test.ts`) and hook coverage (`tests/component/useIntegrationHealth.test.tsx`).

items.title: AI Reliability & Evaluation Harness
items.notes: Shipped Wave 4 AI reliability closeout: added shared reliability contracts (`shared/types/ai-reliability.ts`), canonical trace/scenario registry (`scripts/ai-reliability-registry.ts`), reliability report (`scripts/report-ai-reliability.ts`, markdown + `--json` + `--soft-gate`), pipeline wiring advisory report (`scripts/report-ai-pipeline-wiring.ts`), trace-map docs (`docs/testing/ai-reliability-pipeline-trace-map.md`), and new contract/unit/integration coverage (`tests/contract/ai-pipeline-trace-map.test.ts`, `tests/unit/ai-reliability-harness.test.ts`, `tests/integration/ai-critical-domain-reliability.test.ts`). Coverage now traces schema, content brief/review, SEO assist, client decision support, diagnostics, admin chat, client search chat, and brand/voice provenance-sensitive paths.

items.title: Data Integrity & Recovery Drills
items.notes: Shipped 2026-05-15: added repeatable data integrity and recovery tooling (npm run verify:data-integrity) with SQLite quick/integrity checks, foreign-key violation reporting, workspace-orphan detection across workspace-scoped tables, explicit cross-table consistency checks, and a canonical artifact preserve-vs-rebuild map. Added recovery runbook notes covering backup/restore drills and restore-based migration rollback flow, plus unit coverage for healthy and corrupted database scenarios.

items.title: Observability as Product Infrastructure
items.notes: Shipped 2026-05-15: added platform observability telemetry capture for operation traces, slow-route tracking, AI outcomes, external provider outcomes (SEMRush/DataForSEO), and job lifecycle traces. Added typed workspace observability contracts and report builder in shared and server modules, workspace-scoped API endpoint /api/observability/:workspaceId, and a CLI report via npm run verify:observability. The report covers failed jobs, external API failure rates and latency, AI cost and latency by feature, slow-route leaderboards, and critical sync last-success timestamps. Added workflow docs and integration coverage for the endpoint.

items.title: Release Safety Checklist & Smoke Flow
items.notes: Shipped 2026-05-15: added a practical release-safety workflow with roadmap-derived deploy notes and a repeatable checklist/report script (`npm run verify:release-safety`) covering feature-class release gates, staging smoke suite, rollback readiness, feature-flag rollout, and a post-release monitoring window. items.title: Recurring Permission & Tenant Boundary Audit

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — Platform Health Wave 3 — Silent-Failure Reduction Rails
sprints.rationale: Reduce correct-looking but miswired code by centralizing serializers, events, AI operation contracts, external fetch behavior, and workspace mutation lifecycle rails.

items.title: Client-Safe Serializer Layer
items.notes:
Create explicit serializers for high-value admin/client/public views such as toPublicWorkspaceView(), toClientInboxItem(), toClientSchemaView(), and toAdminSchemaView(). Goal: stop route handlers from hand-picking fields inline and reduce regressions where the backend stores a field but public/client endpoints forget to expose it.
Shipped 2026-05-14 in PR #734: added explicit client-safe/public/admin serializers across workspace/public, inbox, and schema read paths with contract coverage so route handlers no longer hand-pick field lists inline.

items.title: Domain Event Definitions
items.notes:
Layer a richer event registry over WS_EVENTS that documents event name, owning domain, producer routes/services, payload shape, query keys to invalidate, admin/client listeners, and related activity type. This should prevent drift where a broadcast exists but no listener handles it, or a listener expects a payload field that one emitter does not send.
Shipped 2026-05-14: added `scripts/platform-domain-event-definitions.ts` as the canonical WS event registry (owning context, producer/listener surfaces, invalidation hints, activity mappings), added advisory report CLI (`scripts/report-domain-event-definitions.ts`, markdown + --json), and unit coverage for full WS_EVENTS key coverage, stable report shape, and orphan producer/listener mapping detection.

items.title: AI Operation Registry
items.notes:
Build a registry for AI operations that declares feature/domain, provider/model, prose vs structured JSON, response schema/parser, research-mode expectations, cost-tracking key, timeout/retry policy, and whether the operation may run synchronously or must use the background job platform. This extends callAI() from a dispatcher into an auditable contract surface.
Shipped 2026-05-14: added server/ai-operation-registry.ts as the canonical contract map, extended callAI() to accept operation ids with registry-derived defaults (provider/model/feature/retry/timeout/responseFormat/researchMode), and wired representative high-value flows (content brief regenerate, schema plan, client search chat, content decay, keyword strategy synthesis, workspace context generation jobs). Added unit + contract coverage for registry integrity, dispatcher default/override behavior, and representative operation wiring.

items.title: Server External Fetch Helper
items.notes:
Create shared helpers for external/public web fetches and provider API fetches. Standardize timeouts, redirect behavior, user-agent/headers, safe URL handling, error classification, expected network TypeError handling, and logging. Migrate high-risk sitemap/page/image/provider fetch call sites first.
Shipped 2026-05-15: expanded `server/external-fetch.ts` with `fetchPublicWeb*`, `fetchProvider*`, and `fetchExternalBytes` helpers; added URL safety modes, DNS/private-address SSRF guards, redirect-chain revalidation, and explicit unsafe/timeout/network/http classification. Migrated high-risk call sites in semrush, dataforseo provider, sales audit, web scraper, rewrite-chat load-page, webflow alt-text image downloads, webflow-pages sitemap reads, and internal-links fetch paths. Added unit coverage for redirect-to-private, DNS-to-private, and IPv6-mapped loopback (`::ffff:7f00:1`) blocking plus integration coverage updates for rewrite-chat load-page safety behavior.

items.title: Workspace Mutation Helper
items.notes:
Introduce a lightweight runWorkspaceMutation-style helper for repeated mutation lifecycle pieces: workspace scoping, optional read-before-write context, transaction boundary, activity logging, broadcast event, error shape, and serialized response. Avoid over-abstracting Express; target high-churn workspace-scoped writes where missing activity/broadcast bugs are expensive.
Shipped 2026-05-15: promoted `runWorkspaceMutation` beyond the content-plan pilot by migrating `server/routes/client-actions.ts` admin/client mutation paths (create, admin update, public respond) to the shared lifecycle helper with route-specific error mapping and explicit read-before-write guards. Added helper-adoption contract coverage (`tests/contract/workspace-mutation-helper-adoption.test.ts`) and refreshed integration helper coverage naming (`tests/integration/workspace-mutation-helper-pilot.test.ts`) to keep the pattern auditable in high-churn workspace-scoped writes.

items.notes:
PR #711 landed the shared SEO Editor write-target contract/resolver and CMS approval apply support, but staging smoke found product-quality issues in the unified surface: duplicated Sent to Client panels, unscoped approval history, CMS summaries that may already be truncated at source, and missing effective-title fallback semantics when a CMS item name is the practical SEO title. Follow-up hotfix gated the unified shell behind `seo-editor-unified` (default off) so the legacy Pages / CMS Collections split remains active while these issues are resolved.
Shipped 2026-05-15: resolved unified-surface quality issues by rendering a single SEO-scoped `PendingApprovals` panel in unified mode (while disabling nested duplicates in static/CMS child sections), preserving scoped approval visibility. Added CMS effective-title fallback semantics so blank SEO-title fields fall back to item name in both write-target resolution and preview rendering. Removed extra UI truncation in CMS approval history/value summaries to avoid compounding already-truncated source values. Added/updated contract and unit coverage (`seo-editor-unified-flag-gate`, `seo-write-target-resolver`) for the new unified panel and fallback behavior.

items.title: Route-to-Service Extraction Pilot
items.notes:
Choose one bounded context, preferably Schema or Inbox, and move business logic out of route files into server/domains/<domain>/ while preserving all existing URLs, response shapes, broadcasts, activity logs, and tests. Treat this as a proof of the organization pattern before expanding it.
Shipped 2026-05-15: completed an Inbox route-to-service extraction pilot by introducing `server/domains/inbox/client-actions-mutations.ts` and delegating admin create/update + public respond mutation orchestration from `server/routes/client-actions.ts` into the domain module while preserving route URLs, response shapes, activity logging, broadcasts, and side effects (email + playbook enqueue). Also promoted `runWorkspaceMutation` helper ownership to `server/workspace-mutation-helper.ts` (with route shim compatibility) and refreshed the mutation-helper adoption contract to enforce helper usage on the extracted inbox domain module. Added extraction contract coverage (`tests/contract/inbox-route-service-extraction.test.ts`) plus targeted route/integration verification for behavior parity.

items.title: Content Post — Statistics Provenance Check
items.notes: Two-part improvement to reduce hallucinated statistics in generated content. (1) Brief-grounding constraint: during brief generation, extract and tag specific statistics found in scraped SERP/competitor/knowledge-base sources as verifiedStats[]. Inject a generation-time instruction to only cite numbers present in the brief research; use directional language ('significantly faster') when no sourced number exists. (2) Post-generation provenance flag: after unification pass, regex-match all numeric claims in the output and cross-reference against the brief research text. Any number not traceable to a source gets flagged in the PostEditor review checklist for human verification before publish. No automated fact-checking; the goal is surfacing the risk to the reviewer, not replacing judgment.

items.title: Feature tool monoliths - split SchemaSuggester, PageIntelligence, SeoEditor, VoiceTab
items.notes: Verified still valid on origin/staging eb0a5832. SchemaSuggester, PageIntelligence, SeoEditor, and VoiceTab remain >1,000-line feature modules. PageIntelligence slices on 2026-05-06 have extracted pure display/scoring helpers, data derivation helpers for persisted-analysis hydration/search/sort/fix-queue ranking, the pages-tab header/control strip, the pages list/expanded detail renderer, page row/detail renderers, expanded detail strategy/analysis/persisted-summary/action sections, and parent workflow hooks for page-analysis jobs, keyword editing/tracking, and SEO copy state with unit/source contract coverage; see FEATURE_AUDIT entries 344-350. SchemaSuggester generation workflow extraction now moves saved snapshot/page-type/page-picker/fix-context scan state and the shared schema-generator background job handling into useSchemaSuggesterGeneration, with source contract coverage and typed BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES.SCHEMA_GENERATOR startup; see FEATURE_AUDIT entry 351. SchemaSuggester CMS workflow extraction now moves CMS template page loading/generation/publish/copy state, schema CMS field-mapping query/mutation cache updates, and detected location/service mapping collection derivation into useSchemaSuggesterCmsWorkflow with source contract coverage; see FEATURE_AUDIT entry 352. SchemaSuggester publishing workflow extraction now moves page publish/retract, client approval submission, manual-delivery, schema edit/copy, template-save, bulk publish, and edit-state refresh state into useSchemaSuggesterPublishingWorkflow with source contract coverage; see FEATURE_AUDIT entry 353. SchemaSuggester results rendering extraction now moves schema impact display/fetch state plus page/validation/edit-status summary rendering into SchemaImpactPanel, SchemaResultsSummary, and SchemaEditStatusSummary with unit/source contract coverage; see FEATURE_AUDIT entry 354. SchemaPageCard details extraction now moves existing-schema badges, validation finding grouping, recommendation banners, graph summaries, generation diagnostics, and rich-result eligibility into SchemaPageCardDetails with component coverage; see FEATURE_AUDIT entry 355. SchemaSuggester setup extraction now moves business-profile callout, generator hero actions, initial page type picker/guide, and CMS field mapping rendering into SchemaGeneratorSetup with component coverage; see FEATURE_AUDIT entry 356. Schema monolith cleanup is complete; stop here before SeoEditor and VoiceTab per sprint direction. SeoEditor decomposition resumed on 2026-05-07 with Phase 1 contract/type extraction (PR #602), Phase 2 derived-helper extraction for approval payload assembly plus page filtering/ranking logic (PR #603), Phase 3 bulk-helper extraction for pattern preview/apply payloads, bulk rewrite job request payloads, and static-page SEO/openGraph update payload assembly (PR #605), Phase 4 persistence-helper extraction for session cache/draft/job-id hydration and guardrail coverage (FEATURE_AUDIT entry 365), Phase 5 approval-workflow hook extraction (FEATURE_AUDIT entry 366) with filtered select-all mismatch hardening, Phase 6 page-workflow hook extraction for save/draft/rewrite/analyze orchestration (FEATURE_AUDIT entry 367), Phase 7 bulk-workflow hook extraction for analyze-all/bulk-fix/pattern/rewrite orchestration with page-state clear tracking consolidation and source contract coverage (FEATURE_AUDIT entry 368), Phase 8 shell-controls extraction for tracking summary/analyze controls/CMS toggle/search composition hardening (FEATURE_AUDIT entry 369), Phase 9 header-action extraction for stats/refresh/fix/publish/action-banner composition hardening (FEATURE_AUDIT entry 370), Phase 10 page-list extraction for CMS empty-state/banner and PageEditRow composition hardening (FEATURE_AUDIT entry 371), Phase 11 workflow-panels extraction for pending approvals/unsaved banner/SEO suggestions/bulk-operations composition hardening (FEATURE_AUDIT entry 372), and Phase 12 session-state hook extraction for cache hydration/persistence, fix-context auto-expand, and local expanded/preview state composition hardening (FEATURE_AUDIT entry 373). VoiceTab decomposition resumed on 2026-05-07 with Phase 1 model/helper extraction that moved shared constants/defaults/mappings plus normalized dedupe helpers into `src/components/brand/voice-tab/voiceTabModel.ts`, added source-contract guardrails, and covered duplicate/whitespace edge cases in unit tests (FEATURE_AUDIT entry 374). VoiceTab Phase 2 now extracts the samples and DNA sections into `src/components/brand/voice-tab/SamplesSection.tsx` and `src/components/brand/voice-tab/DNASection.tsx`, preserving mutation/toast behavior while adding source-contract coverage to keep section ownership out of the root shell (FEATURE_AUDIT entry 375). VoiceTab Phase 3 now extracts guardrails and calibration surfaces into `src/components/brand/voice-tab/GuardrailsSection.tsx`, `src/components/brand/voice-tab/CalibrationSection.tsx`, and `src/components/brand/voice-tab/useVoiceCalibrationWorkflow.ts`, with source-contract coverage and preserved generate/refine/feedback/sample flows (FEATURE_AUDIT entry 376). VoiceTab Phase 4 now extracts profile query/mutation/tab-section orchestration into `src/components/brand/voice-tab/useVoiceTabShell.ts` with source-contract coverage that locks root-shell composition boundaries (FEATURE_AUDIT entry 377). With VoiceTab shell cleanup merged, this feature-tool monolith split item is now complete. Final closeout on 2026-05-08 also completed CmsEditor phases 1-7 and PageRewriteChat phases 1-7 (PRs #627 through #636), so this umbrella platform-consolidation split item is now fully done.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — Deep Bug Remediation
sprints.rationale: Follow-up queue from docs/superpowers/audits/2026-05-04-deep-bug-audit.md. Focuses on systemic correctness issues found after PR #440: workspace/resource scoping, test isolation, path identity joins, client contract drift, content invalidation, and AI provenance.

items.title: Security scoping PR 1 - Webflow/site ownership guard
items.notes: Added shared requireWorkspaceSiteAccess guard proving workspace -> Webflow site ownership before token lookup or resource mutation. Applied it to Webflow asset/page/publish routes, CMS collection item read/write/publish routes, organize/rename routes, schema site routes, Smart Name, SEO copy, and guarded Webflow job/content-publish surfaces. Threaded workspaceId through scoped admin callers and added cross-workspace JWT integration tests plus owned-site positive-path coverage for schema validation, Smart Name, and SEO copy.

items.title: Test isolation - per-suite DB plus ROI page_keywords read path
items.notes: Added per-worker isolated Vitest DATA_DIR setup before DB singleton import, made spawned integration servers inherit the worker DATA_DIR, shared awaited child-process shutdown across raw-spawn suites, and extended the port uniqueness meta-test to raw server PORT constants. ROI now reads normalized page_keywords rows as source of truth with legacy pageMap fallback, covered by a public ROI integration test that strips legacy pageMap.

items.title: Security scoping PR 2 - Google, reports, global lists, settings, billing, uploads
items.notes: Hardened Google/GSC/GA admin data routes with workspace-site and GSC-property ownership checks, made global Google/settings writes require HMAC admin auth, filtered workspace list and overview responses for scoped JWT users, guarded audit report/snapshot/action routes by owning workspace, required workspace access before uploads, and added authenticated-actor-only billing management for passwordless portals. Added targeted scoped-JWT and passwordless-route regression tests across Google, reports, settings, billing, uploads, and workspace list/overview.

items.title: Page identity joins - normalize remaining page/profile/chat/recommendation lookups
items.notes: Replaced remaining fuzzy/raw page identity joins with exact shared identity helpers across admin chat pageMap lookup, workspace intelligence SEO-context/insights/page-profile joins, audit/CWV/schema/link/SEO edit/content-decay matching, and frontend recommendation filtering. Added frontend/server helper parity tests plus regression coverage for homepage identities, full URLs with query/hash, nested pages, and sibling leaf paths like /seo vs /services/seo.

items.title: Client API contract drift - trials, strategy enrichment, delivery links, priorities
items.notes: Use computeEffectiveTier in all public tier-gated routes, expose content-gap enrichment fields used by the client strategy UI, include deliveryUrl/deliveryNotes for delivered/published public content requests, and align client business priority storage with ClientSignalsSlice reads. Added contract/integration tests for each producer-consumer pair.

items.title: Content data flow - broadcasts, activity, and derived cache invalidation
items.notes: Shipped 2026-05-04: added a centralized content:updated workspace event for content-planning/content-pipeline mutations and wired it through the shared WS invalidation bus. Content templates, matrices, content-plan review actions, briefs, post generation/regeneration/revert/delete, and publish-related handlers now refresh admin pipeline/calendar/home/intelligence/ROI plus client content plan/content request caches. Added user-visible activity entries for template/matrix/brief/post lifecycle actions, fixed read-before-delete for brief/post/template/matrix deletes, made client-flagged content-plan cells a real shared status, and added route coverage proving flagged cells stay visible and activity is recorded.

items.title: AI provenance contracts - factual checklist, AEO citations, response parsing

Added route, component, and unit coverage for the contracts.

items.title: Content Gap Opportunity Score (Item 8)
items.notes: Shipped 2026-04-23. computeOpportunityScore() exported from server/routes/keyword-strategy.ts. Scores content gaps 0–100 by composing volume (45% weight, capped at 10K/mo), ease-of-ranking via 1−KD/100 (45% weight), and a GSC impressions additive bonus (up to 5% of the raw score, capped at 2K impressions). A trend multiplier (×1.3 rising / ×0.7 declining / ×1.0 stable) is applied last. Results sorted descending in both admin ContentGaps.tsx and client StrategyTab.tsx with blue score badges.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — Broadcast Invalidation Audit (do next)
sprints.rationale: Recurring UX symptom: components show stale/empty data on first mount and 'fix themselves' on hard refresh. PageIntelligence is the confirmed instance; likely a platform-wide class of bug.

items.title: Fix PageIntelligence missing broadcast invalidation
items.source: User report 2026-04-21 — 'page intelligence doesn't display data at first, but then on refresh it does'
items.notes: Shipped 2026-04-21 alongside Devin-flag fixes for PR #249. Added useWorkspaceEvents subscription to src/components/PageIntelligence.tsx that invalidates queryKeys.admin.keywordStrategy on STRATEGY_UPDATED broadcasts. Resolves staleTime 10min cache-miss UX where users saw empty data until hard refresh. #597 (platform-wide audit) still pending.

items.title: Platform-wide audit: useQuery hooks with staleTime but no broadcast subscription
items.notes: Shipped 2026-04-21. Centralized 7 workspace-scoped events in src/hooks/useWsInvalidation.ts (STRATEGY_UPDATED, BRANDSCRIPT_UPDATED, DISCOVERY_UPDATED, VOICE_PROFILE_UPDATED, BRAND_IDENTITY_UPDATED, BLUEPRINT_UPDATED, BLUEPRINT_GENERATED — total centralized now 43). Removed redundant inline useWorkspaceEvents subscriptions from 10 component files (PageIntelligence, SeoEditor, KeywordStrategy, VoiceTab, IdentityTab, BrandscriptTab, DiscoveryTab, PageStrategyTab, BlueprintDetail, BlueprintVersionHistory). Added two-layer regression guardrail: pr-check rule (self-syncing allowlist parsed from useWsInvalidation.ts, escape hatch `// ws-invalidation-ok`) catches reintroduced inline handlers; contract test (tests/contract/ws-invalidation-coverage.test.ts) catches new WS_EVENTS entries added without either centralization or an explicit LOCAL_ONLY_EVENTS rationale. Blueprint queries rely on React Query prefix invalidation (['admin-blueprint', wsId] / ['admin-blueprint-versions', wsId]).
